author: codex-agent

patch.value: Strengthen SDK typing for extension registration contracts

SDK/extension audit found extension-facing registration types that are too loose (Record<string, unknown> style payloads), which weakens autocomplete and compile-time validation for schema/search/service extension authors and increases trial-and-error integration.
SDK exports concrete extension registration interfaces for schema/search/service payloads, examples compile with those types, and extension authoring docs reflect the typed contracts.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm context --limit 10; pm search 'Extension SDK schema type definitions Record unknown' --limit 10; pm search 'SchemaFieldDefinition Record unknown ExtensionApi typing' --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Net-new because existing open SDK issue pm-1etl covers module resolution/diagnostics,not compile-time contract precision for extension registration payloads.

message: Track SDK typing hardening finding from comprehensive dogfood audit

value.note: Extension API registration typing surface audited for loose payload contracts
SDK export surface reviewed for extension authoring DX
Extension authoring docs to align with stronger SDK type contracts

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: SDK audit evidence: src/core/extensions/loader.ts currently aliases multiple extension registration contracts as Record<string, unknown> (SchemaFieldDefinition, SchemaItemTypeDefinition, SearchProviderDefinition, VectorStoreAdapterDefinition), which limits compile-time validation and editor guidance for extension authors. Recommend promoting these to explicit interfaces in SDK exports.

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Fresh SDK audit evidence 2026-04-26: external-style TypeScript smoke using defineExtension, ExtensionApi, and CommandDefinition compiled successfully after including required manifest priority. pm contracts --flags-only exposed all 52 PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES with no missing core commands; remaining DX gap is still the loose Record<string, unknown> aliases for FlagDefinition, SchemaFieldDefinition, SchemaItemTypeDefinition, SchemaMigrationDefinition, SearchProviderDefinition, and VectorStoreAdapterDefinition.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: 2026-04-26 audit confirms SDK gaps: defineExtension is minimal, field/search/vector types are Record<string,unknown>, no extension scaffold template exists, no deactivate lifecycle hook. SDK exports are clean and well-documented in EXTENSIONS.md.

author: codex-agent

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Replaced loose extension registration records with explicit SDK interfaces and exported them for extension authors, with docs and starter examples updated.
Extension authors receive compile-time contracts for schema/search/vector/service registration and corresponding SDK documentation/examples.
Typed contracts are available through sdk exports, starter example/docs added, and SDK regression suites pass.
